title: Remote agents in Vibe. Powered by Mistral Medium 3.5. | Mistral AI
gist: Mistral released Mistral Medium 3.5, a 128B open-weight merged reasoning/coding/instruction model, and moved its Vibe coding agent from local laptop execution to asynchronous, parallel cloud-run agents integrated across Le Chat, GitHub, Linear, Jira, Sentry and Slack/Teams, alongside a new general-purpose 'Work mode' agent.

insights.claim: Mistral released Mistral Medium 3.5, a 128B-parameter dense model that merges instruction-following, reasoning, and coding into a single set of weights, released as open weights under a modified MIT license.
insights.speaker: Mistral Blog
insights.so_what: Mistral continues to differentiate on open-weight distribution even for its flagship model, undercutting closed-weight competitors on enterprise self-hosting flexibility.
insights.quote: Mistral Medium 3.5, a new flagship model that merges instruction-following, reasoning, and coding into a single 128B dense model. Released as open weights, under a modified MIT license.

insights.claim: Mistral Medium 3.5 can be self-hosted on as few as four GPUs and has a 256k token context window, with reasoning effort configurable per request so the same model handles quick replies or complex agentic runs.
insights.speaker: Mistral Blog
insights.so_what: Low hardware footprint (4 GPUs) targets enterprises wanting on-prem/private deployment without hyperscaler-scale GPU clusters, a key wedge against larger closed models that require far more inference infrastructure.
insights.quote: It is a dense 128B model with a 256k context window, handling instruction-following, reasoning, and coding in a single set of weights. It performs strongly in real-world use, with self-hosting possible on as few as four GPUs.

insights.claim: Mistral trained the Medium 3.5 vision encoder from scratch to handle variable image sizes and aspect ratios, giving the model native multimodal capability alongside text-to-text, reasoning, coding and agentic tasks.
insights.speaker: Mistral Blog
insights.so_what: Native multimodal support signals Mistral is targeting document/UI-heavy agentic workflows (e.g., Work mode's cross-tool tasks), not just text coding use cases.
insights.quote: We trained the vision encoder from scratch to handle variable image sizes and aspect ratios.

insights.claim: Mistral Medium 3.5 scores 77.6% on SWE-Bench Verified, which Mistral states places it ahead of its own prior model Devstral 2 and of Qwen3.5 397B A17B, and it scores 91.4 on the τ³-Telecom agentic benchmark.
insights.speaker: Mistral Blog
insights.so_what: Mistral is directly benchmarking against Alibaba's Qwen line as well as its own prior coding model, positioning Medium 3.5 as competitive with leading open-weight coding models on SWE-Bench Verified.
insights.quote: Mistral Medium 3.5 scores 77.6% on SWE-Bench Verified, ahead of Devstral 2 and models like Qwen3.5 397B A17B. It also has strong agentic capabilities and scores 91.4 on τ³-Telecom.

insights.claim: Mistral Medium 3.5 replaces Devstral 2 as the coding model in Vibe CLI and becomes the new default model across Le Chat, effectively retiring its predecessor coding model within weeks of its prior release.
insights.speaker: Mistral Blog
insights.so_what: Rapid internal model iteration cadence signals Mistral is prioritizing coding/agentic capability as its lead competitive vector against OpenAI, Anthropic and Google.
insights.quote: Mistral Medium 3.5 becomes the default model in Le Chat. It also replaces Devstral 2 in our coding agent, Vibe CLI.

insights.claim: Mistral Medium 3.5 is priced at $1.5 per million input tokens and $7.5 per million output tokens via API, and is also hosted on NVIDIA GPU-accelerated endpoints on build.nvidia.com and distributed as an NVIDIA NIM containerized inference microservice.
insights.speaker: Mistral Blog
insights.so_what: The NVIDIA NIM distribution deal extends Mistral's reach into enterprise infra already standardized on NVIDIA tooling, an alternate go-to-market channel beyond Mistral's own API and Le Chat.
insights.quote: Through API, it’s priced at $1.5 per million input tokens and $7.5 per million output tokens. Open weights are on Hugging Face under a modified MIT license.

themes.theme: Shift of coding agents from local to cloud

insights.claim: Mistral moved its Vibe coding agents from running locally on a developer's laptop to running asynchronously and in parallel in the cloud, where sessions notify the user on completion instead of requiring step-by-step supervision.
insights.speaker: Mistral Blog
insights.so_what: This directly targets the same async cloud-coding-agent market as GitHub Copilot workspace, OpenAI Codex cloud agents and Devin, positioning Mistral as a full-stack competitor rather than just a model provider.
insights.quote: Coding agents have mostly lived on your laptop. Today we're moving them to the cloud, where they run on their own, in parallel, and notify you when they're done.

insights.claim: An ongoing local Vibe CLI coding session can be 'teleported' to the cloud mid-task, carrying over session history, task state, and approvals, so developers are not forced to choose upfront between local and remote execution.
insights.speaker: Mistral Blog
insights.so_what: The seamless local-to-cloud handoff reduces switching friction versus competitors that require separate cloud-only or local-only agent products, a potential retention lever for developer workflows.
insights.quote: Ongoing local CLI sessions can be teleported up to the cloud when you want to leave them running, with session history, task state, and approvals carrying across.

insights.claim: Each Vibe cloud coding session runs in an isolated sandbox for broad edits and installs, and on completion the agent can open a GitHub pull request and notify the user, shifting human review from every keystroke to the final result.
insights.speaker: Mistral Blog
insights.so_what: Sandboxed execution plus PR-based review is the emerging standard pattern for enterprise-safe autonomous coding agents, reducing the trust barrier to broader adoption.
insights.quote: Each coding session runs in an isolated sandbox, including broad edits and installs. When the work is done, the agent can open a pull request on GitHub and notify you, so you review the result instead of every keystroke that produced it.

insights.claim: Vibe integrates into existing enterprise toolchains including GitHub and pull requests, Linear and Jira for issues, Sentry for incidents, and Slack or Teams for reporting, and Mistral states this capability was originally built for its own in-house coding environment before being extended to enterprise customers and now to all users.
insights.speaker: Mistral Blog
insights.so_what: Deep integration with the standard enterprise dev-ops stack (Jira/Linear/Sentry/Slack) is a moat-building move that increases switching costs once adopted by engineering teams.
insights.quote: It plugs into GitHub for code and pull requests, Linear and Jira for issues, Sentry for incidents, and apps like Slack or Teams for reporting.

themes.theme: Work mode: general-purpose agent expansion beyond coding

insights.claim: Mistral launched Work mode in Le Chat, a new agentic mode powered by Mistral Medium 3.5 that acts as the execution backend for the assistant, reading and writing across multiple tools in parallel to complete multi-step tasks like cross-tool workflows, research synthesis, inbox triage, and Jira issue creation.
insights.speaker: Mistral Blog
insights.so_what: This extends Mistral's ambitions from a coding-agent vendor to a general enterprise productivity/agentic-workflow platform, competing with Microsoft Copilot and Google Workspace's AI agents rather than just GitHub Copilot.
insights.quote: Work mode is a powerful new agentic mode for complex tasks in Le Chat, powered by a new harness and Mistral Medium 3.5. The agent becomes the execution backend for the assistant itself.
insights.locator: New Work mode in Le Chat (Preview)

insights.claim: In Work mode, data connectors to documents, mailboxes, calendars and other systems are enabled by default rather than chosen manually, letting the agent pull context automatically, though Le Chat still requires explicit user approval before sensitive actions like sending a message or modifying data.
insights.speaker: Mistral Blog
insights.so_what: Default-on connectors increase agent usefulness but also raise data-governance and permissioning stakes for enterprise buyers evaluating Mistral against more cautious competitors.
insights.quote: In Work mode, connectors are on by default rather than chosen manually, which lets the agent reach into documents, mailboxes, calendars, and other systems for the rich context it needs to take correct action.
insights.locator: New Work mode in Le Chat (Preview)
